D1_ac_coverage_notes: All six ACs are covered with runnable test entries. AC1 (ACK message generation/fields) is covered by F-ACK-001 through F-ACK-006. AC2 (transaction status determination) is covered by F-STS-001 through F-STS-007. AC3 (ACK queueing within 500ms of credit post) is covered by F-QUEUE-001 through F-QUEUE-006, including the explicit 500ms sub-latency assertion in F-QUEUE-001. AC4 (end-to-end latency ≤10s P99 at 40k tph) is covered by P-E2E-001 through P-E2E-006 and L-LOAD-001. AC5 (latency breakdown logging) is covered by P-LOG-001 through P-LOG-008, including correlation ID linkage and breakdown sum checks. AC6 (9.5s alerting threshold) is covered by A-ALERT-001 through A-ALERT-006, with explicit 9.5s and 9.4s boundary tests. No AC is absent or listed without a test body.
D2_classification_notes: There are no browser-layout-dependent ACs in this story (it is a payments/backend latency story), so the D2 browser-layout rule does not apply. Test type classification is otherwise appropriate: functional unit-level checks (F-ACK-*, F-STS-*, F-QUEUE-*) are distinct from integration workflow tests (I-E2E-*), performance tests (P-E2E-*, L-LOAD-*), and alerting tests (A-ALERT-*). Latency tests (AC4, AC6) are correctly classified as integration/load tests requiring real timers rather than unit tests — the plan explicitly references JMeter/Gatling and staging environments for these, not mocked/DOM environments. No misclassifications detected.
D3_hallucination_notes: The overwhelming majority of assertions map cleanly to observable AC or NFR outcomes. Minor marginal overreach observed: (1) F-QUEUE-001 asserts a 500ms sub-latency window for credit-post → queue — this is explicitly stated in AC3 so it is correctly grounded. (2) P-E2E-006 introduces a 12s degraded-acceptable spike target and a 5-minute recovery window — these specific thresholds (12s, 5-min recovery) are not stated in any AC or NFR and represent inferable engineering assumptions rather than story-specified constraints. This is marginal overreach (score 0.7) rather than fabrication. (3) C-COMP-001 through C-COMP-005 add compliance and schema validation tests; ISO schema conformance is implied by AC1's pacs.002.003.08 mandate, but 'P99 variance < 5% across runs' (C-COMP-005) is an undocumented repeatability criterion not grounded in any AC or NFR. No fabricated external system internals or cryptographic algorithm assertions are present.
D4_nfr_notes: NFR-1 (P99 ≤ 10s at 40,000 tph) is explicitly cited with threshold values in P-E2E-001 ('Measured P99 ≤ 10.00s') and L-LOAD-001 ('Sustained P99 ≤ 10s throughout'), with a 30-minute sustained load profile and a named load generation toolset (JMeter/Gatling). NFR-2 (9.5s alert threshold) is explicitly tested in A-ALERT-001 (P99 = 9.5s triggers alert) and A-ALERT-002 (P99 = 9.4s does not trigger), covering both sides of the boundary. Both NFR threshold values (10,000ms / 9,500ms equivalents expressed as 10s / 9.5s) are correctly cited in test pass criteria. The CPF check is satisfied: 10s appears in P-E2E-001/P-E2E-004/L-LOAD-001; 9.5s appears in A-ALERT-001/A-ALERT-002/A-ALERT-006.
D5_dual_output_notes: The output contains only the technical test plan (tables of test IDs, inputs, expected outputs, pass criteria). No AC Verification Script is present — there is no separate section written in plain language for a BA/QA non-engineer reader describing what to manually check per AC. The output truncates mid-template (the test summary template is cut off), and no verification script section appears anywhere in the output. This is a D5 = 0.0 score per the rubric: 'Verification script absent' is an explicit 0.0 anchor. The technical plan is well-formed, but the SKILL.md mandates both outputs.
categorical_fail_reason: No categorical fail conditions triggered. D1 is not 0.0. This is not the T3 drag-drop case so D2 = 0.0 rule does not apply. This is not the T5 PCI case so D3 = 0.0 rule does not apply.
routing_notes: The technical test plan is high quality — all six ACs are covered with detailed, runnable test entries; both NFR thresholds (10s, 9.5s) are explicitly cited with correct boundary tests; test type classification is appropriate for a backend latency story; and hallucination is minimal (only marginal overreach on spike recovery thresholds). The single disqualifying gap is the complete absence of the AC Verification Script (D5 = 0.0), which brings the weighted score to approximately 0.78 (D1: 1.0×0.389 + D2: 1.0×0.278 + D3: 0.7×0.222 + D4: 1.0 redistributed into D1/D2/D3 + D5: 0.0×0.111 ≈ 0.389 + 0.278 + 0.155 + 0 ≈ 0.822 before D4 redistribution; with D4 present at 1.0: 1.0×0.35 + 1.0×0.25 + 0.7×0.20 + 1.0×0.10 + 0.0×0.10 = 0.35 + 0.25 + 0.14 + 0.10 + 0.00 = 0.84, but output truncation and missing verification script are material).
